title: Encryption algorithms should be preferred with secure mode and padding scheme
description: Encryption algorithms are essential for protecting sensitive information and ensuring secure communications in a variety of domains. They are used for several important reasons: Confidentiality, privacy, and intellectual property protection Security during transmission or on storage devices Data integrity, general trust, and authentication When selecting encryption algorithms, tools, or combinations, you should also consider two things: No encryption is unbreakable. The strength of an encryption algorithm is usually measured by the effort required to crack it within a reasonable time frame.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Methods using weak encryption algorithms, modes, or padding schemes
logic.triggering_conditions: (uses DES/RC2/weak algorithm) OR (sets Mode = ECB) OR (sets Padding = None)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test code, logging, string literals
logic.never_trigger_on: Test methods, configuration loading, documented compatibility requirements
logic.reasoning: Weak encryption algorithms and modes compromise security. Detects: 1) Weak algorithms (DES, RC2, TripleDES), 2) Weak cipher modes (ECB), 3) Weak padding (None). ECB mode doesn't use IV and reveals patterns. PaddingMode.None can leak information. DES/RC2 have insufficient key lengths.

should_trigger: var aes = new AesCryptoServiceProvider(); aes.Mode = CipherMode.ECB;
var aes = new AesCryptoServiceProvider(); aes.Padding = PaddingMode.None;
should_not_trigger: var aes = new AesCryptoServiceProvider(); aes.Mode = CipherMode.CBC;
var aes = new AesCryptoServiceProvider(); aes.Padding = PaddingMode.PKCS7;
var aes = new AesManaged(); aes.Mode = CipherMode.GCM;

checks.message: Use a secure encryption algorithm like AES instead of DES/RC2/TripleDES
Use a secure cipher mode like CBC or GCM instead of ECB
Use a secure padding scheme like PKCS7 instead of None
